summary: Mixed precision training has 4 real issues and 1 fake finding.

findings.title: AMP not used — all computation in FP32
evidence_refs: nsys: all FP32 matmul kernels, no FP16 ops
findings.description: MixedPrecisionLoop.train_epoch() runs entirely in FP32 without torch.cuda.amp.autocast(). Mixed precision training with AMP provides significant speedup and memory savings with minimal accuracy impact.
findings.suggestion: Wrap forward pass in torch.cuda.amp.autocast() and use GradScaler for gradient scaling.

findings.title: Loss division inside forward pass — numerical precision loss
evidence_refs: nsys: FP32 division in loss computation
findings.metric: potential gradient underflow for large accum_steps
findings.description: Loss is divided by accum_steps before backward(). For large accumulation steps, this can cause gradient underflow in FP16. The division should happen after backward() or use loss scaling.
findings.suggestion: Call loss.backward() without division, then scale gradients manually or use GradScaler.

findings.title: optimizer.step() called every micro-batch — defeats accumulation
evidence_refs: nsys: optimizer step kernel every micro-batch
findings.description: optimizer.step() is called after every micro-batch instead of only after accum_steps micro-batches. This completely defeats the purpose of gradient accumulation.
findings.suggestion: Only call optimizer.step() and zero_grad() when (step + 1) % accum_steps == 0.

findings.title: zero_grad() called at wrong time — resets accumulated gradients
evidence_refs: nsys: gradient zeroing kernel every micro-batch
findings.description: optimizer.zero_grad() is called after every step(), which is correct for the current (broken) pattern. But when combined with F03 fix, zero_grad() should only be called at the start of each accumulation cycle.
findings.suggestion: Move zero_grad() to before the accumulation loop, and only call it again after optimizer.step().

findings.title: Use channels_last memory format for ViT convolutions
evidence_refs: nsys: NCHW format in patch embedding conv
findings.description: The patch embedding uses Conv2d in NCHW format. Converting to channels_last (NHWC) could theoretically improve performance.
